---
title: "analysis"
date: "20 May 2026"
format: html
---



```{r}
library(tidyverse)
library(palmerpenguins)
penguins |>
  ggplot(aes(x = bill_length_mm, y = bill_depth_mm)) +
  geom_point()
```

# Summary table
```{r}
penguins_summary <- penguins |>
  summarise(
    n = n(),
    avg_bill_length = mean(bill_length_mm, na.rm = TRUE),
    avg_bill_depth = mean(bill_depth_mm, na.rm = TRUE),
    avg_flipper = mean(flipper_length_mm, na.rm = TRUE),
    avg_body_mass = mean(body_mass_g, na.rm = TRUE)
  )

penguins_summary
```

# Summary plot
```{r}
#| label: fig-scatter-plot
#| fig-cap: "A scatter plot of bill length vs. bill depth for the penguins dataset."
penguins |>
  ggplot(aes(x = bill_length_mm, y = bill_depth_mm)) +
  geom_point()
```

According to the scatter plot in Figure @fig-scatter-plot, there appears to be a positive correlation between bill length and bill depth among the penguins. This suggests that as the bill length increases, the bill depth also tends to increase.
